Level 311 is the 312th level of The Backrooms.

== Description ==
Level 311 appears as a vast, mostly empty maze spanning in all directions. Consisting of rooms made of stone with several unlit torches on the walls, the layout is similar to that of [[Level 0]]s. Although these torches are unlit, light still seems to be coming from them. The floor is made of a carpet like material, and is filled with a strange substance that one should never smell or consume, and the walls reek of a stench similar to that of decaying matter.

{{Blurimage|align=right|file=EmptySlate.jpg|caption=An image of Level 311.}}

Upon entering the level, it will begin to constantly change its shape and layout. The wanderer will usually never notice, but will eventually recognize that something is off,this makes it almost impossible to go back to where you came from, as it will have changed by the time you get back to your original position. Due to this anomaly, no maps have been made of this level, along with there being no clear or set path to the exit.

It is recommended not to stay in this level for too long, as after staying here for an undocumented amount of time the wanderer will start to get a migraine. About an hour after this migraine, the host will grab any sharp object in their sight and will begin to mindlessly walk to the residence of the only documented entity of this level, the hive mind. The wanderer will then kneel down to the hive mind, after which they will begin to repeatedly stab themselves in the chest until they pass out. The hive mind will then merge the remains of the wanderer with its physical body, making the deceased wanderer a permanent part of the entity.

On rare occasions, one can find stashes of almond water and fully prepared meals in this level. However the contents of these consumables appear to be filled with Liquid Pain, rendering them dangerous to ingest.

Wandering far enough into the level will cause it to change its appearance to a well furnished living area, while still keeping the same stone maze appearance mentioned before. The layout of these sections resemble that of the halls of a hotel, and there are several numbered doors that each lead to different levels depending on which one was opened. Many people have tried to stay in these sections of the level, however due to the effects this level has on the wanderer, it is recommended to only stay here for a small amount of time as a resting spot to avoid injuries or death.

Some parts of this level look similar or even almost identical to the original Level 0 despite this level not being affiliated with said level in any way, shape, or form. Before this level was discovered, it was theorized that this was a sublevel or section of Level 0 due to parts of said level being in this one, however this was debunked due to the fact that there are no confirmed entrances to this level from Level 0.

==Section 2==
Section 2 takes the appearance of a medieval fortress with several interconnected rooms and features. There are 3 main rooms located in this section, each with their own unique features. The hive mind's influence cannot reach into this section of the level, therefore making this section of the level easier to navigate due to not having to worry about how much time has passed.

=== The Lobby ===
{{Blurimage|align=right|file=CastleLobby.jpg|caption=A photo of the lobby.}}
The Lobby is the main area of section 2. It consists of stone, brick walls and several chandeliers, along with doorways that lead to the other rooms of this section. Torches are lined up on the walls, and the carpet is dry and soft. Most passageways lead to either entirely empty rooms, or rooms that are run down and abandoned. An anomalous effect occurs in this section if one tries to wander too far into the lobby. Their vision will get foggy, and they will pass out and wake up at the start of the lobby. Because of this, the lobby has yet to be fully documented. 
=== The Armory ===
{{Blurimage|align=left|file=The Armory.jpg|caption=An image of the armory.}}The Armory appears as a small medieval style room with pillars and tables found in it. Several stands with weapons and armor can also be found here, and a single ceiling light hangs in the middle of the room. The weaponry here appears to be made of a metal originating from the frontrooms called titanium, and makes the weapons  almost completely indestructible. The same cannot be said for the armor, as it is made out of copper. The armor and weapons can be taken off their stands and used in battle. Because of this, many wanderers have been seen fighting each other, often resulting in fatal injuries or even death.

=== The Temple ===
{{Blurimage|file=TempleThing.webp|caption=An image of the temple.}}The Temple takes the appearance of a small portion from an ancient egyptian temple. The floor is partially covered in sand, pillars line the walls and corridors, and hieroglyphics are depicted on the walls. As of now, these hieroglyphics have yet to be translated. This part of section 2 was not present until recently, so it is unknown how or why it is here in the first place. Due to the little information we have about this room, it has not been fully documented yet, and it is unknown if any danger is present in it or not.

.

.

== Entities ==
{{Entity Class|no=Hive-Mind|Hab=Level 311|Agg=4|Int=2|Dan=4}}

There is only one entity present in this level, going by the name of the hive mind. The hive mind takes the appearance of a large human heart surrounded by corpses of unknown origin. Around 101~ minutes into the level; an effect known only as 'Assimilation' will occur; the person affected will walk towards the Hive Mind, before attaching themselves to a nearby vine, commiting suicide. The victims bodies almost always decompose within 30 minutes of death.

The Hive Mind has also been known to attack using its vines; using them to hang victims.<ref>It will only attack the un-assimilated.</ref>

== Colonies and Outposts ==
In section 2's armory room, a group of four people can be found going by the name of "The Squires". The Squires are wanderers wielding the armor and weapons that can be found in said room, and are usually described by other wanderers to be very kind people. They will usually give away supplies to wanderers who pass by, but only if they are willing to engage in battle with them. The Squires tend to go easy on a wanderer when engaging in battle; so that they don't accidentally kill the wanderer.

== Entrances and Exits ==

=== Entrances ===

* Certain hallways in [[Level 343]] may fade into section 1 of this level.
* Abandoned buildings in [[Level 11]] will send one here.
* The road of [[Level 50]] may transition into stone, leading one here.

=== Exits ===

* Numbered doors in certain parts of this level will lead to levels 0-50 depending on which door was opened.
* If the walls start to become white, you may be transported to [[Level 12]].
* No-clipping into an armor stand in the armory room will send you to [[Level 54]]
* [[Level 959|Don't.]]
